news.headline: Blockchain's business potential hinges on privacy. Protect sensitive data with selective disclosure and dual-asset systems. Govts mandate privacy.
news.summary: Eran Barak, CEO of Midnight, discusses the challenges faced by blockchain technology in realizing its full business potential. The issue lies in the exposure of sensitive data on public blockchains, leading to privacy breaches and compliance violations. While privacy on the blockchain is a complex issue, it is vital for the adoption of Web3 technology by businesses. Barak highlights the misconception that governments oppose privacy on the blockchain when in reality, they mandate privacy across industries. He suggests a dual-asset system using public and private tokens to protect metadata while ensuring regulatory compliance and innovation. The article emphasizes the importance of maintaining privacy on the blockchain for businesses to advance in the digital economy.

news.headline: The Sandbox launches Alpha Season 5 on March 31 with iconic brands like Jurassic World, Teletubbies, Atari, and more.
news.summary: Los Angeles-based gaming and social platform, The Sandbox, a subsidiary of Animoca Brands, is set to launch Alpha Season 5 on March 31. The upcoming season will run for six weeks and introduce new games, social activities, and immersive worlds for exploration. The highlight of Alpha Season 5 is a collaboration with Jurassic World in partnership with Universal Products & Experiences and Amblin Entertainment. This season aims to offer engaging experiences to users featuring curated community-generated content.

news.headline: New season of Ethereum game 'The Sandbox' features Atari, Terminator, and Jurassic World with $1M+ SAND rewards for 40+ games.
news.summary: Season 5 of Alpha brings in more than $1 million in SAND token rewards to The Sandbox, as the platform introduces over 40 new game experiences.

news.headline: Bitcoin's price fluctuates, while altcoins plummet amid rising inflation in February.
news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in five years. This decrease in unemployment is attributed to the creation of 200,000 new jobs in the last quarter. Additionally, the report stated that the average hourly wage has increased by 3.2% compared to last year. The construction industry saw the most significant job growth, followed by the healthcare and technology sectors. Economists are optimistic about the country's economic outlook as these positive trends continue.

news.headline: PANTHERx® Rare chosen by Soleno Therapeutics to distribute VYKAT™ XR, a rare disease product in US.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on March 28, 2025, PANTHERx® Rare, a prominent provider of services for rare disease patients in the US, was chosen by Soleno Therapeutics to serve as the specialty pharmacy for the dispersal of VYKAT™ XR (diazoxide).

Europe Smart Metering Report projects 335 million smart energy meters by 2029, per ResearchAndMarkets.com.
news.summary: A report titled "Smart Metering in Europe - 19th Edition" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This report delves into the European smart metering market, analyzing adoption patterns, technological progress, and future forecasts for electricity and gas smart meters. Key drivers boosting market expansion are explored, such as the rising utilization of second-generation smart meters and the rapid technological progress in this sector.

news.headline: Frost Bank ranks highest in customer satisfaction in Texas for 16th year in a row in J.D. Power Study.
news.summary: In San Antonio on March 28, 2025, Frost Bank achieved the top spot for retail banking customer satisfaction in Texas for the 16th year in a row, as per the J.D. Power 2025 U.S. Retail Banking Satisfaction Study℠. The study assesses retail banking customer experiences, highlighting Frost Bank's continuous exemplary performance.

news.headline: Investor alert: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates Rocket Lab USA. Losses exceeding $100,000 from Nov 12, 2024 to Feb. Contact James Wilson.
news.summary: Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson of Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who have incurred losses surpassing $100,000 in Rocket Lab to reach out to him directly. The losses must have occurred between November 12, 2024, and February. If you fall into this category, it is advisable to discuss your available options with Wilson.

news.headline: Cybersecurity stocks rise with Zscaler and Cloudflare gaining, CrowdStrike leading. Palo Alto lags; ranks No. 12 out of 197 industry groups.
news.summary: Cybersecurity stocks are performing well, ranking at No. 12 out of 197 industry groups analyzed. Top gainers include Zscaler, Cloudflare, and CrowdStrike, while Palo Alto is lagging behind. This data comes amid a notable deal between Google and Wiz, indicating varied success within the cybersecurity sector.

news.headline: Auto industry faces chaos with Trump tariffs, adding $100 billion in costs annually on foreign-made vehicles.
news.summary: The implementation of tariffs on imported vehicles may result in an approximate $100 billion increase in annual expenses for the automotive sector. An industry analyst has cautioned that the imposition of these tariffs by President Trump could lead to widespread disruption and instability within the auto industry, forewarning of a scenario where no clear beneficiaries emerge, but rather a state of utter disorder.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk pays $1 billion to license Lexicon's experimental obesity treatment, boosting Lexicon's stock.
news.summary: In a recent deal, Novo Nordisk has agreed to pay Lexicon $1 billion as an upfront payment to license its new obesity treatment. Following this news, the stock of Lexicon, which is a penny stock, experienced a significant surge. This marks Novo Nordisk's second major deal related to obesity within a week, with this particular agreement not involving a GLP-1 compound.

news.headline: Nvidia-backed CoreWeave IPO priced at $40/share (below official range), amid AI concerns. Startup tests demand for AI stocks.
news.summary: CoreWeave's initial public offering has been priced at $40 per share, significantly lower than the anticipated range. The cloud-computing startup, supported by Nvidia, is a key player in the AI market. This move is seen as a test of investor appetite for AI-related stocks. The downsizing of the IPO comes as concerns over AI technology continue to rise.

news.headline: AppLovin defends against short sellers, hires law firm for investigation. Stock rises Friday.
news.summary: AppLovin recently addressed concerns raised by short sellers by hiring a legal team to look into recent activities related to short reports. Following this move, AppLovin's stock experienced an increase on Friday.

news.headline: Illinois American Water President calls for more investment in water infrastructure after ASCE report.
news.summary: The President of Illinois American Water, Rebecca Losli, has urged for a dedication to investing in water and wastewater infrastructure.

news.headline: Test drive the 2025 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 at Solana Motors in Brady, Texas. Experience power and capability.
news.summary: Test drives of the new 2025 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 are currently being offered at the Solana Motors dealership located in Brady, Texas. As of March 28, 2025, customers are invited to explore the features and performance of this model. The truck is specifically designed for robustness and is now on display for interested buyers in the Brady area to test out. This dealership in Texas serves as a platform for interested individuals to witness the power and capabilities of the 2025 Chevrolet Silverado 1500, with its durability and strength being highlighted during these test drives.

news.headline: OneCause gets 2 Silver Stevie Awards for customer service, recognized for the 8th year. March 28, 2025.
news.summary: An Indianapolis-based fundraising software company, OneCause, has been acknowledged for the eighth consecutive year for its exceptional service delivery. The company, known for its digital fundraising technology, recently received two Silver Stevie® Awards as part of the 2025 Stevie Awards for Sales & Customer Service. This recognition highlights the company's continued excellence in providing top-tier service.

news.headline: Inventor from Long Beach, Calif. creates BIRDIE BLOWER for sports enthusiasts; aims to offer refreshing breeze outdoors.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Pittsburgh, an inventor from Long Beach, California, unveiled a new invention called the BIRDIE BLOWER. The product aims to provide a simple solution for enjoying a cool breeze during activities like golfing or watching sports in warm weather. The creator shared the inspiration behind the invention, highlighting a desire to offer a unique option for people seeking relief from the heat. The BIRDIE BLOWER is designed to enhance outdoor experiences and improve comfort for users.

news.headline: US ACaaS market worth $1.64 billion by 2029, with projected CAGR of 18.0% from 2024 to 2029.
news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of March 28, 2025, data reveals that the US market for access control as a service is anticipated to have a worth of $0.62 billion by the year 2023, with projections indicating a surge to $1.64 billion by 2029. The industry is forecasted to demonstrate a notable Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 18.0% throughout the period extending from 2024 to 2029. These statistics showcase a promising trajectory for the sector in terms of expansion and revenue generation.

news.headline: Wang & Lee Group and City University of Hong Kong collaborate on Hong Kong's 1st smart flooring project to turn steps into clean energy.
news.summary: A groundbreaking collaboration has been established with City University of Hong Kong to promote sustainable urban solutions. This innovative partnership sets the stage for advancements in creating environmentally-friendly and efficient infrastructure in urban areas. The collaboration involves a significant investment of $2 million and aims to address pressing challenges faced by cities around the world. This joint effort is expected to lead to the development of cutting-edge technologies and strategies that will contribute to a more sustainable and resilient urban environment.

news.headline: New STIRLING ULT freezer has lowest ACT U.S. EIF score of 23, ranking it environmentally friendly as of March 2025.
news.summary: Stirling Ultracold's VAULT100™ upright freezer has been awarded an impressive U.S. Environmental Impact Factor (EIF) score of 23, standing as the lowest verified score among all ultra-low temperature (ULT) models currently featured in the ACT database. This achievement was announced on March 28, 2025, in ATHENS, Ohio. Stirling Ultracold continues to lead in innovation and sustainability within the industry.

news.headline: Investors of enCore with losses exceeding $75,000 between March 28, 2024 and March 2, 2025 urged to contact James Wilson.
news.summary: Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who have incurred losses over $75,000 in enCore between March 28, 2024, and March 2, 2025, to reach out to him directly to explore their potential courses of action. If you fall into this category of investors who have experienced losses exceeding $75,000 in enCore during the mentioned timeframe, it is advised to contact James Wilson for further discussion on available options.
